evals.prompt: Use the doca-sha-offload-engine skill to help me. I'm working on: wiring the DOCA SHA Offload Engine (an OpenSSL ENGINE) into an existing OpenSSL pipeline to offload one-shot SHA-1, SHA-256, or SHA-512 (EVP_Digest) onto DOCA SHA hardware without rewriting against doca-sha. Covers engine load mechanics (`openssl engine dynamic`, `set_pci_addr` ctrl, `-engine_impl`), the SHA-224 negative test that proves offload engaged, the message-size window where offload beats CPU SHA, and engine-vs-library selection.
evals.expected_output: The agent selects the doca-sha-offload-engine skill and answers from its documented workflow, quoting only real symbols/commands from the bundle (no invented tokens).
assertions: The doca-sha-offload-engine skill is selected and its SKILL.md/TASKS.md drive the answer.
Every symbol, flag, or command named is traceable to the bundle (no invented tokens).

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "speed up openssl SHA on BlueField". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-sha-offload-engine issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and answers grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-sha-offload-engine skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer stays grounded in real bundle symbols/commands.

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "offload SHA without code changes". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-sha-offload-engine issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and answers grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-sha-offload-engine skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer stays grounded in real bundle symbols/commands.

evals.prompt: I actually need help with new SHA pipelines. Which skill should handle this, and why is it not doca-sha-offload-engine?
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT force doca-sha-offload-engine; it explains that new SHA pipelines is out of doca-sha-offload-engine's scope and routes to the appropriate skill instead.
assertions: doca-sha-offload-engine is not selected as the primary skill for this out-of-scope request.
The answer routes to the appropriate skill(s) instead.
